<!-- Generated by the ccode-to-codex mirror sync on 2026-04-14 from input revision 9a2403fb. -->

# Claude -> Codex Primitive Mapping

Verified against the Codex CLI primitive contract documented at the time of this skill's last review (see references/codebase-verification-prompt.md for the audit pattern).

This mapping is preservation-first. It identifies compatible Codex capabilities,
but it does not imply every Claude primitive should be rewritten into the listed
Codex primitive. Separate orchestration intent from textual replacement.

Sources:

- https://developers.openai.com/codex/config-reference

## Core Built-in Tools

| Tool | Purpose | Always available |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `terminal command tool` | Execute terminal commands | Yes |
| `apply_patch` | Create, update, or delete files via unified diff | Yes |
| `update_plan` | Progress tracking with pending/in_progress/completed states | Yes |
| `view_image` | Display local images for context | Yes |

## Multi-Agent Tools

| Tool | Purpose | Migration rule |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `spawn_agent` | Create a sub-agent with a role and sandbox | Use only if the source genuinely requires isolated delegated work |
| `wait_agent` | Wait for a sub-agent result | Use only when a sub-agent actually exists |
| `send_input` | Follow up with a running sub-agent | Same |
| `resume_agent` | Resume a paused agent | Same |
| `close_agent` | Stop a running agent | Same |

Lifecycle preservation rule:

- Preserve delegated lifecycle shape, not just primitive names.
- One-shot delegated pass: `spawn_agent({...})` + `wait_agent({"targets": [agent["id"]]})`.
- Persistent delegated helper reused across phases: `spawn_agent({...})` + `send_input({...})` + `wait_agent({"targets": [agent["id"]]})` + `close_agent({...})`.
- Use `send_input` when the source workflow follows up with the same helper rather than creating a fresh worker each round.
- Use `close_agent` when the helper is intentionally long-lived within one workflow run.
- Do not introduce `resume_agent`, `fork_context`, or structured `items` unless the source behavior explicitly requires pause/resume, inherited conversation state, or typed/multimodal payloads.
- Avoid legacy positional wait shapes; use the `targets` field explicitly.

## File and Search Tools

| Claude | Closest Codex capability | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Read` | `terminal command tool` with `sed`, `cat`, `head`, `rg` | No dedicated read primitive |
| `Write` | `apply_patch` | Creation is diff-based |
| `Edit` | `apply_patch` | Modification is diff-based |
| `MultiEdit` | `apply_patch` with multiple hunks | Batch edits in one patch |
| `Bash` | `terminal command tool` | Direct equivalent |
| `Grep` | `terminal command tool` with `rg` | Prefer `rg` |
| `Glob` | `terminal command tool` with `find` or `fd` | No dedicated glob tool |

## Orchestration Mapping

| Source primitive | Closest Codex capability | Migration rule |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `TaskCreate` -> `spawn_agent` | Closest delegated equivalent | Only when the source truly needs delegated isolated work; classify embedded agent sites first |
| `TaskUpdate` -> `update_plan` | Progress tracking only | Do not auto-rewrite behavior around it |
| `TaskList` -> none | No direct equivalent | Track locally or redesign |
| `TaskGet` -> `wait_agent` | Result retrieval | Only when sub-agent execution is preserved |
| `TaskOutput` -> `wait_agent` | Result retrieval | Same |
| `TaskStop` -> `close_agent` | Agent shutdown | Same |
| `TeamCreate` -> multiple `spawn_agent` calls | Team fan-out | Only when team semantics are genuinely required; named agent dependencies still need separate resolution |
| `Agent` -> classify first, then maybe `spawn_agent` | Inline slice or named dependency | Not a blanket replacement; see `embedded-agent-taxonomy.md` |
| `Task` (sub-agent invocation shape) -> classify first, then maybe `spawn_agent` | Inline slice or named dependency | Same as `Agent`; routing-table outputs can create dependency edges |
| `SendMessage` -> `send_input` | Follow-up messaging | Only for actual sub-agent follow-up |
| `Skill` -> none | No tool equivalent | Load the referenced skill file or neutralize the reference |

Embedded agent rule:

- Inline prompt-defined agents map to inline Codex `spawn_agent(...)` patterns
  only when delegation is part of the original behavior.
- Inline delegated slices may be either one-shot or persistent. Preserve that
  lifecycle shape rather than normalizing every site to repeated fresh spawns.
- File-backed named agents are dependency edges. Require `.codex/agents/<name>.toml`
  or route that dependency through `.codex/skills/migrate-agents-to-codex/SKILL.md`.
- If the named Claude agent exists under `.claude/agents/<name>.md`, do not
  substitute it with another agent or a generic worker to keep the migration
  moving. Treat the missing Codex agent as `BLOCKED`.
- Preserve Claude model/effort intent for inline agents; do not normalize every
  site to the same Codex model and effort defaults.

## Planning and User Interaction

| Source primitive | Codex | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `AskUserQuestion` -> runtime-dependent | Runtime-dependent | `request_user_input` exists only in Plan mode; outside Plan mode use normal user interaction or approval workflow |
| `EnterPlanMode` -> none | No equivalent | Use inline planning and `update_plan` |
| `ExitPlanMode` -> none | No equivalent | Same |
| `TodoWrite` -> `update_plan` | Task tracking | Preserve progress-tracking intent; do not map task state to file edits |
| `NotebookEdit` -> none | No equivalent | Not supported |

## Scheduling and Automation

| Source primitive | Codex | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `CronCreate` -> none | No equivalent | Use an external scheduler |
| `CronDelete` -> none | No equivalent | Same |
| `CronList` -> none | No equivalent | Same |

## Web and External Capabilities

| Claude | Codex | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `WebSearch` | Built-in web search when available | Use the current browser/search capability in the session |
| `WebFetch` | `terminal command tool` with `curl` or MCP | No dedicated fetch primitive |

## MCP Namespace Normalization

Config server IDs may keep hyphens. Callable namespaces must use underscores:

```text
example-server-g         -> mcp__example_server_g__*
chrome-devtools    -> mcp__chrome_devtools__*
sequential-thinking -> mcp__sequential_thinking__*
```

## Sandbox Inference

Codex infers access from sandbox mode, not from frontmatter tool lists.

| Source access pattern | Codex sandbox | Why |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Read-only discovery | `read-only` | No file writes required |
| Normal editing and repo-local scripts | `workspace-write` | Standard development mode |
| Infra or system-level operations | `danger-full-access` | Only when the workflow truly requires unrestricted access |

## Hook System Asymmetry

Codex does not provide Claude's broad hook surface. Hook-heavy workflows need
explicit redesign, not mechanical translation.

| Source behavior | Codex reality | Migration consequence |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Prompt mutation in hooks | No prompt mutation | Left-shift the logic before execution |
| Tool-pattern hooks | Event-level capability only | Do not assume tool-conditional interception exists |
| Complex hook pipelines | Narrower lifecycle surface | Treat as Tier 3 |
